design_philosophy.core_loop: Select one of three ninja androids with distinct playstyles (slow/powerful, quick/weak, balanced), navigate linear side-scrolling stages, defeat waves of enemies and bosses using attacks, grabs, blocks, dashes, and a screen-clearing power meter that builds over time
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management of power meter (builds slowly, drains on knockdown) versus enemy positioning and attack patterns; character selection creates distinct difficulty curves based on player preference
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as an untested combat android ninja tasked with assassinating a tyrant dictator; three distinct ninja archetypes allow players to approach combat differently (raw power vs speed vs balance)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Tight, arcade-style controls with asymmetric character design; refusal to include co-op despite being a beat-em-up (single-player only with character swapping on continue); power meter that fully drains on knockdown creates momentum-based risk/reward; environmental hazards and interactive objects add physics-based problem solving alongside combat

difficulty.continues_system: Unlimited continues with ability to swap character selection on continue
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; completing a stage progresses the player forward; no mid-stage checkpoints documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through character selection (Ninja for power, Kamaitachi for speed, Kunoichi for balance); power meter management creates skill expression; anti-piracy measures in Western versions severely punish detected piracy by disabling level select, nullifying health pickups, preventing health refill between stages, and forcing restart to intro on death

progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight stages progression unlocked sequentially; hidden level select available (X+Y at title screen with specific button sequence); hidden sound test available (L+R); character selection available upfront
replayability_hooks: Three distinct characters with unique movesets encourage replays to experience different combat approaches
Character swap on continue allows mid-playthrough strategy changes
Environmental hazards and interactive objects (motorcycles, safes) add environmental puzzle elements
Power meter risk/reward creates moment-to-moment decision making

world_building_techniques: Opening scroll text establishes dystopian setting: tyrannical Banglar, brainwashed populace, underground resistance led by Mulk, androids as last resort
Character selection screen establishes three android archetypes with ninja styling
Stage progression through enemy territories (mutant soldiers, combat robots, environmental hazards) reinforces militaristic setting
Ending sequence reveals darker thematic layer: androids self-destruct to prevent threat, but Mulk's new government develops stronger androids, suggesting cycle of tyranny repeats

Regional narrative differences: Western versions softened assassination language and removed female Kunoichi enemies (replaced with small male creatures), indicating censorship-driven world-building adjustments

boss_design.philosophy: Linear stage progression culminates in boss encounters; bosses serve as stage-end gates reflecting enemy archetypes (mutants, cyborgs, military leaders); final boss is Banglar himself, the narrative antagonist
notable_bosses: Banglar the Tyrant (final boss) - dwarfish mutant-cyborg dictator commanding global superpower

faction_indicators: Banglar's regime: brainwashed human soldiers, vicious mutants, non-sentient combat robots
Mulk's resistance: underground revolutionaries fighting for liberation
Power shift: androids serve liberation initially, but Mulk's post-victory government perpetuates militarism
danger_signals: Dictatorial control through brainwashing (loss of independent thought)
Escalating military power (Banglar's forces, then Mulk's android army)
Cycle of tyranny: ending reveals new regime threatens to become as oppressive as the old
Untested combat technology deployed under pressure (androids released before testing complete)
environmental_storytelling: Stages progress through military installations and enemy territory, showing regime's reach
Hazards like minefields and armed helicopters establish militarized landscape
Interactive environmental objects (safes, motorcycles) suggest infrastructure and logistics of oppressive regime
Eight-stage structure represents journey through layers of regime resistance to reach Banglar's fortress

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - The Ninja Warriors SNES page)
wikipedia (The Ninja Warriors 1994 video game article)
